description_long: This object is a rectangular control cabinet with a slightly sloped front face, featuring an array of labeled buttons, switches, and rotary dials. The front panel is densely packed with tactile controls, each clearly marked with text and symbols, suggesting use in a technical or industrial environment such as a power distribution or communication hub. The cabinet is constructed from a solid, matte-finished metal, with side panels that are smooth and unadorned. The rear and side views reveal a robust, boxy enclosure with no visible openings, handles, or ventilation grilles. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing durability and ease of access to the control interface. The color scheme is predominantly gray, with lighter gray buttons and white labeling for high visibility.
description: A robust, gray metal control cabinet with a sloped front panel densely populated by labeled buttons and switches, designed for technical or industrial use.
description_view_0: The front view shows the sloped control panel with numerous labeled buttons, switches, and dials, all clearly visible and accessible.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the solid, smooth side panel with no visible features or openings.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a plain, boxy back with no visible access points, handles, or ventilation.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing a featureless, smooth panel.
